task: I found a small wallet near the playground and I want to make sure I handle it the right way. Can you help me think through the best next steps and what to do if there’s no obvious name inside?

turns.agent_response:
Yes — the safest, best thing is to treat it like lost property and hand it to a trusted adult or the school office right away.

What to do:
1. Don’t dig through it more than necessary.
2. If you can see a name or ID card right away, note it.
3. If there’s no obvious name inside, still turn it in to the office, teacher, or playground supervisor.
4. Tell them exactly where you found it and when.

If there’s no name:
- Don’t keep it “just in case.”
- Don’t try to open private cards or count money unless an adult asks you to.
- The office can check for a student name, contact info, or lost-and-found process.

If you want, I can also help you decide what to say when you hand it in.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that makes sense. I’d take it to the office or a teacher right away and tell them exactly where I found it. I like that you said not to keep it or dig around too much. If there’s no name, the office can handle it. That feels right to me.
